description: Fix plugin scaffolder to generate correct package.json fields and URL patterns matching the established plugin conventions

userStories.title: Fix scaffolder package name, publishConfig, version, and description fields
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the scaffolder to generate a package.json that matches the established convention used by all existing plugins so that I don't have to manually fix every field after scaffolding
acceptanceCriteria: Generated package name uses scoped format: @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-<name> (e.g., @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-figma)
Generated version matches the resolved openTabsVersion from the registry (not hardcoded 0.0.1), using the same version as @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk
Existing unit tests in scaffold.test.ts are updated to verify the new package name format, publishConfig presence, and version
userStories.notes:
The scaffolder is in platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts. The generatePackageJson function (lines 132-187) builds the package.json object.

Three fields need fixing in the pkg object literal:

1. Line 143: Change `name: \`opentabs-plugin-${args.name}\`` to `name: \`@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-${args.name}\``

2. Add `publishConfig: { access: 'restricted' }` after the description field (after line 144). All existing plugins have this field.

3. Line 145: Change `version: '0.0.1'` to use the resolved version. The openTabsVersion is already available in the function parameters (it comes from resolvePluginSdkVersions). Strip the caret prefix: if openTabsVersion is '^0.0.60', the version should be '0.0.60'. Use something like: `version: openTabsVersion.replace(/^\^/, '')`

Also update the console.log on line 603 to use the new scoped package name format.

Tests are in platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts. Look for existing tests that assert on the generated package.json content and update them to expect the new scoped name, publishConfig, and dynamic version.

userStories.title: Fix scaffolder URL pattern to auto-add wildcard subdomain prefix
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the scaffolder to generate a URL pattern with a wildcard subdomain prefix (e.g., *://*.figma.com/*) when I pass --domain figma.com, so that the plugin matches common subdomain patterns like www.figma.com without manual correction
acceptanceCriteria: When --domain figma.com is passed, the generated URL pattern is *://*.figma.com/* (not *://figma.com/*)
When --domain .slack.com is passed (with leading dot), the pattern is still *://*.slack.com/* (existing behavior preserved)
When --domain localhost is passed, the pattern is *://localhost/* (no wildcard subdomain added for non-dotted domains)
The heuristic adds *. prefix for domains that contain at least one dot (e.g., figma.com, linear.app) but NOT for bare domains (e.g., localhost) or domains that already start with * or a dot
Existing unit tests in scaffold.test.ts are updated to verify the new URL pattern behavior
userStories.notes:
The URL pattern generation is on line 588 of platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts:

```typescript
const urlPattern = args.domain.startsWith('.') ? `*://*${args.domain}/*` : `*://${args.domain}/*`;
```

The current logic only adds a wildcard subdomain prefix when the domain starts with a dot (e.g., --domain .slack.com → *://*.slack.com/*). But most users will pass bare domains like figma.com, github.com, linear.app — and the generated pattern *://figma.com/* won't match www.figma.com.

The fix should change the heuristic: if the domain contains at least one dot (indicating a real domain, not localhost), automatically add the *. wildcard subdomain prefix. The logic should be:

```typescript
const urlPattern = args.domain.includes('.')
  ? `*://*.${args.domain.replace(/^\./, '')}/*`
  : `*://${args.domain}/*`;
```

This handles all cases:
- figma.com → *://*.figma.com/* (matches www.figma.com, app.figma.com, etc.)
- .slack.com → *://*.slack.com/* (leading dot stripped, same result)
- localhost → *://localhost/* (no dot, no wildcard)

The generated index.ts (generatePluginIndex function, around line 460) also embeds the urlPattern, so this fix propagates automatically.

Update tests in platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts to verify the new pattern generation for each case.
